title: Operational affordance lane
goal: Implement open affordance issues #556-#559 and #562-#564 so common package workflows expose obvious primary actions, structured next steps, and lower context burden.
non_goals: Do not add new first-line surfaces when existing commands can be reshaped.
Do not make the shipped package depend on GitHub, Codex, this repository, or a specific language/tool host.

intent.outcome: Make operational surfaces self-routing by affordance while preserving compact startup and progressive disclosure.
intent.constraints: Prefer shrinking, merging, or action-object shaping over new commands or prose handbooks.
intent.latitude: Implement child issues in the smallest slices that preserve tests and package neutrality.
intent.escalation: Stop if the next useful change changes product direction, requires a new top-level surface, or hardens repo-specific workflow into package contract.

execution.milestone: review guidance, first-contact/action objects, lifecycle/residue/aid routing
execution.next_step: No further lane work remains; this plan is archived as proof.
execution.proof: Use agentic-workspace proof for changed paths plus targeted pytest/ruff; run make check before final closeout.

invariants: compact router views remain first-contact surfaces
raw planning, memory, contracts, and review artifacts remain behind selectors unless directly needed

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Make the package streamlined, lightweight, frictionless, cheap, modular, and small while preserving leverage.

required_continuation.owner surface: closed issue set #556-#559 and #562-#564 plus archived plan
required_continuation.activation trigger: reopen only if a new affordance issue or validation regression appears

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Operational outputs now expose compact primary actions for review guidance, first-contact/proof/closeout, lifecycle mutation review, durable-residue routing, and agent-aid discovery.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Generated CLI roadmap and unrelated backlog.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Agent-aid recommendation lists should stay bounded so selected report sections do not grow into duplicate metadata dumps.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: #557, #558, #559, #562, #563, and #564 focused and selected proof passed.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: make check, issue closeout, and active-plan archive/prune

intent_interpretation.literal request: Ingest and implement the new issues #556+ on the affordances lane.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Use the package to execute the open affordance lane and discover ways to reduce future context overhead.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Promote #556 and children into one active execplan, implement children in order, close issues with proof, and route any context-overhead findings.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Check that changed outputs make the next correct action cheaper without adding repo-specific assumptions.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: docs, tools/skills, .agentic-workspace planning artifacts, src/agentic_workspace, tests, package payload files as needed
execution_bounds.max changed files: keep each milestone small; pause before broad rewrites
execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed <paths> --format json; targeted pytest/ruff; make check before final closeout
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: large CLI extraction or new command framework
execution_bounds.stop before touching: unrelated issue lanes or generated multi-language CLI roadmap

stop_conditions.stop when: all child issues are implemented, proved, closed, and the plan is archived or pruned from active state
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: a child requires a new first-contact surface or product-direction decision
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: work starts adding capability instead of compressing/routing existing surfaces
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: validation cannot prove the affordance claim with local tests or command output

context_budget.live working set: issue list, this execplan, relevant command output, touched source/tests
context_budget.recoverable later: full issue bodies, docs/operational-affordance-design.md, archived plans, review history
context_budget.externalize before shift: active child, changed paths, proof run, context-size friction signals
context_budget.pre-work config pull: agentic-workspace start/report/summary already provide resolved invocation and router state
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: only use memory-router if a child needs durable prior knowledge not present in compact router output
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Resume from this plan, active child issue, git status, and proof selector output.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: large output shape change, validation failure, or issue closure boundary

delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Preserve package neutrality and prefer existing surfaces.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: exact output field names, test placement, and documentation phrasing when acceptance criteria stay satisfied.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: a change would alter authority boundaries or add a permanent first-line surface.

immediate_next_action: None; the lane is complete and archived.
invariants: Do not require agents to read raw planning, Memory, review, or contract files for ordinary entry.
Any new action object should use resolved invocation when it includes a command.
Affordance guidance must stay implementation-independent unless it is explicitly repo-local.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Operational surfaces should expose one primary safe next action where possible.
Context-size reduction is an affordance criterion: outputs should route to details instead of dumping them by default.
open_questions_to_close: Which existing fields can carry action objects without adding new first-contact surfaces?
Can durable-residue and agent-aid workflows be made self-routing through existing report/defaults/skills outputs?
validation_commands: uv run ruff check src tests
completion_criteria: #557, #558, #559, #562, #563, and #564 are implemented or explicitly routed with proof.
Issues are closed only after validation and closeout evidence.
Context-size and cognitive-burden improvement opportunities are fixed, routed, or dismissed.

execution_run.handoff source: user request plus GitHub issue intake
execution_run.what happened: #557 added operational-affordance review; #558/#559 added primary action objects to startup/preflight/proof/closeout outputs and localized remaining startup commands; #562/#563/#564 added lifecycle, durable-residue, and agent-aid primary actions.
execution_run.scope touched: docs/contributor-playbook.md; tools/skills/self-improvement-dogfooding/SKILL.md; src/agentic_workspace/cli.py; startup schema; tests; planning state
execution_run.changed surfaces: existing maintainer docs, repo-local skill, compact command outputs, startup schema, focused tests, active execplan
execution_run.validations run: make maintainer-surfaces; uv run pytest tests/test_maintainer_surfaces.py -q; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run ruff check src tests; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py --quiet-success; uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/contracts scripts/check tests/test_structured_file_inventory.py; uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/operational-affordance-lane.plan.json --format json; uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json; make check
execution_run.result for continuation: No continuation remains for the #556 affordance lane; all child issues and parent are closed.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; changes stayed within existing docs, skill, CLI/report outputs, tests, and planning closeout.
finished_run_review.intent served: yes; outputs now route common package workflows through compact primary actions without adding first-line surfaces.
finished_run_review.config compliance: yes; copyable commands use resolved invocation where local config applies.
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low; review-required lifecycle actions now stay non-mutating.
finished_run_review.follow-on decision: no active follow-on; context-size hotspots remain general architecture candidates rather than this lane's unfinished work.

proof_report.validation proof: selected proof passed for current changed paths; full make check still needed before parent closeout
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: make maintainer-surfaces; uv run pytest tests/test_maintainer_surfaces.py -q; uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q; uv run ruff check src tests; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py --quiet-success; uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/contracts scripts/check tests/test_structured_file_inventory.py; uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed src/agentic_workspace/cli.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py .agentic-workspace/planning/execplans/operational-affordance-lane.plan.json --format json; uv run agentic-workspace defaults --section root_cli_authority --format json; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Implement #556+ affordance lane while dogfooding and evaluating context-size burden.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: All child issues and parent issue were closed with proof comments after make check passed.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Make the package cheaper to enter, recover, verify, and trust through compact router views and progressive disclosure.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: compress and route before adding capability.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this reduce context and cognitive burden for future agents without weakening leverage?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: all planned child issues and the parent were implemented, validated, and closed
closure_check.evidence carried forward: this execplan and external issue references
closure_check.reopen trigger: new affordance issue or validation failure

generated_closeout.authority: derived adapter; structured execplan fields remain authoritative

durable_residue.learned constraint: Context-size review should be part of affordance closeout, not a separate handbook.
durable_residue.motivation worth preserving: Future agents should be able to use compact command outputs instead of loading issue threads and raw planning files.
durable_residue.canonical owner now: implemented behavior and tests in commits 3391bad, 62a797b, and 35c29eb
durable_residue.promotion trigger: none for this lane; repeated future friction should route through improvement intake

execution_summary.validation confirmed: make check passed after selected proof and targeted tests.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): no new durable Memory needed; context-size learning was fixed in bounded report action output.

signals found: External work reconciliation identifies untracked issue pressure but does not provide a copyable promotion action for a selected issue range.
Selected report sections can still add context overhead when action lists duplicate full inventory metadata.
signals fixed: #557 now requires operational-affordance review for changed operational surfaces.
#558/#559 compact startup, preflight, proof, and closeout outputs now expose primary action objects with risk, inputs, and next proof where applicable.
Startup feature-tier and skill-routing command fields now honor resolved local invocation.
#562 lifecycle outputs now route review-required flows to a non-mutating dry-run command and no-review dry-runs to apply.
#563 closeout trust now exposes a durable-residue routing action with destinations and proof.
#564 agent-aid discovery now exposes a bounded primary action and no-aid action instead of requiring storage inspection.
signals routed: Evaluate whether broad external-work promotion needs a writer/scaffold helper after final lane closeout.

drift_log: 2026-04-29: Promoted open affordance issues #556-#559 and #562-#564 into one active lane with context-size review.
